info.name: Pidge Integration APIs
info.description:
The Pidge Suite of APIs is designed to provide a high level of control and visibility to upstream systems, creating an incredible experience for businesses and partners alike.

Examples for each API are well documented and available throughout the link.

High-level explanation of each API is on this link itself, and click the **'READ ME'** to understand more about each variable

Reach out to Pidge for access to **Staging Environment credentials and url**

item.name: Core APIs

request.description:
This API is used to get the Token from a valid **Username** and **Password**.

Use the entire 'Bearer {token}' string as an authorization header when making a request.

While tokens are generally unchanging and can be cached, they can get expired due to inactivity or configuration change (e.g. changing default values, webhooks, etc).

Any of the other APIs will give a 401 error. Handle this by hitting this Login API again and generating a new token.

If password is revoked outright, the login API itself will not return data.token in response.

[**READ ME to understand order creation API variables**](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1bkpkhRIIFxs3FnHON0zNrTA2Q1MLrdmYeCW0Oqw6yRQ/edit#gid=292279110)

This API creates an order in the Pidge system in the 'Pending' stage.

If your token is configured to auto allocate, the API will give a 200 response and the order will attempt to allocate and land in 'Manifested' if successful. If for any reason allocation fails, the order will still be created and will remain in 'Pending'

As this API can handle bulk requests as well, the data object in response is always in the format of

**"{source_order_id}: {pidge id}"**

The source_order_id is as passed in the payload, and the pidge id is used for get status and order updation APIs.

source_order_id cannot be duplicated across objects in a single request, but can be duplicated across requests.

response.name: Exhaustive payload - Parent - 2 or more orders
Failed

request.description: [READ ME to understand more about GET API variables and response]() The GET API returns data passed in order creation and additional 'Fulfillment' Object which contains current and past statuses, rider information and locations, and logs. The **'Status' Key** indicates parent level statuses. These include - CANCELLED (0) - Order cancelled (cannot be recovered) \[TERMINAL STATUS\] PENDING (1) - Order created but not processed for fulfillment PROCESSING (2) - Order in processing for captive fulfillment. this is a very temporary status (\~5 seconds when order processed) and can be ignored FULFILLED (3) - Order has been marked for fulfillment and AWB has been manifested. Note: Order status can revert from FULFILLED to PENDING if client 'Unallocates' the order via API or via dashboard. The **Fulfillment object** variable explanation is available in the READ ME. **fulfillment.logs** also contains minor statuses like 'reached location' and additional remarks. These can be implemented for more visibility. **fulfillment.status always refers to current status.** Fulfillment statuses are as follows - CANCELLED - fulfillment has been cancelled. Order reverted to pending CREATED - Order has been manifested OUT_FOR_PICKUP - Executive on way to sender location for pick up REACHED_PICKUP - Executive has reached pickup location PICKED_UP - Executive picked up order IN_TRANSIT - Order at transient location/warehouse - applicable for E-Commerce OUT_FOR_DELIVERY - Order out for delivery to end customer REACHED_DELIVERY - Executive has reached delivery location DELIVERED - Order delivered to end customer. \[TERMINAL STATUS\] UNDELIVERED - Order undelivered; eligible for reattempt/RTO RTO_OUT_FOR_DELIVERY - Order out for return to origin RTO_UNDELIVERED - Return to origin failed RTO_DELIVERED - Order returned to origin \[TERMINAL STATUS\] LOST - Order Lost \[TERMINAL STATUS\] DAMAGED - Order Damaged \[TERMINAL STATUS\] Note that on all major fulfillment statuses, the GET API payload is available to be shared via Webhook.

response.name: Processing
Fulfilled + Created
Fulfilled + Out for Pickup
Fulfilled + Reached Pickup
Fulfilled + Picked up
Fulfilled + Out for Delivery
Fulfilled + Reached Delivery
Fulfilled + Delivered

request.description: Clients can configure their URL on Pidge and get the same data as is available on the GET API. The data keys are exactly the same, but is not inside the 'data' object. The provided example shows the payload, and this API can be simulated once the webhook endpoint is configured.

The Order Cancel API is used to cancel an order entirely.

This API can only be used when status is 'PENDING'. If an order has been FULFILLED and still not 'PICKED_UP', call 'Order Unallocate', revert order to 'PENDING' then cancel.

Order once cancelled not be recovered or processed again.

item.description: These are the main APIs you will need to successfully execute end-to-end flows

item.name: Supporting APIs

request.description:
The Get Serviceability API returns the partners who are currently available to fulfill the order. This API acts as a gateway for 3rd Party Partner APIs and queries serviceability real-time. This API also shares Pidge Powered Partners that offer public networks or private networks where the requesting business has membership to the network.

The token of the partner to be selected needs to be passed in the Fulfill Order API in the body with the order id, partner id, and partner service name. In case of allocaton to self, token and id do not need to be passed.

Please note the following -

- This API is chargeable and will give 4xx in case there is inadequate wallet balance or credit line exceeded. Please contact the Pidge team for pricing.
- The token in response are JWT tokens that are time-bound and id-bound. Please ensure to hit this API in quick conjunction with the fulfill API
- As this is a sync API, the speed of response may vary depending on partner API response.
- Rates provided are real-time and should not be used for caching purposes as they may change at time of actual order creation.

response.name: Success

request.description:
The Fulfill API allows businesses to request fulfillment for orders that may have been placed with a token with manual allocation in the Create Order API.

The body requires the JWT token provided in the GET serviceability API with the order id, service name, and network id. For captive, token does not need to be passed.

Do note that in case of insufficient wallet balance and limit, this API will fail.

The Smart Fulfill API allows for a bypass of the GET serviceability API for fulfillment, native & front-end based customization for functionality in allocation, and the ability to choose the best partners real-time without additional decision-making.

The smart_allocation_id in the API is generated when a smart allocation rule is set on the Pidge dashboard. This rule can be used in the body of this API request.

Smart allocation lets businesses give ranked preference to captive riders and 3rd party partners, and set cost and time limits for partner selection.

Note that if there is insufficient balance for fulfillment, or if none of the partners satisfy the smart allocation preference and criteria, the API will respond with 4xx.

Auto Smart Allocation can also be set in the token at time of token generation or update.

response.name: Failure - No partner found
Failure - Wrong smart_id

request.description:
This API returns the current location of the rider while on order.

In case of completed orders, the API will return an empty response.

Note that this API is rate limited to once per 30 seconds per order, across all IPs. Usage frequency can be increased for an additional charge.

response.name: Error

request.description:
The Unallocate Order API can be used to revert an order from 'FULFILLED' to 'PENDING'.

This API can only be used while status is 'FULFILLED' AND fulfillment.status is in \['CREATED','OUT_FOR_PICKUP','REACHED_PICKUP'\]

Order is reverted to pending and able to be processed again.

The Get Quote API returns the partners who are currently available to fulfill an order for the supplied pickup and drop coordinates. This API acts as a gateway for 3rd Party Partner APIs and queries serviceability real-time. This API also shares Pidge Powered Partners that offer public networks or private networks where the requesting business has membership to the network.

The Get Serviceability still needs to be used for getting the quote against an order ID. The quotation from Get Quote and Get Serviceability may differ basis actual order details and/or time of query.

Please note the following -

- This API is chargeable and will give 4xx in case there is inadequate wallet balance or credit line exceeded. Please contact the Pidge team for pricing.
    
- As this is a sync API, the speed of response may vary depending on partner API response.
    
- Rates provided are real-time and should not be used for caching purposes as they may change at time of actual order creation.

response.name: Success
Fail

request.description:
This API returns the current location of the rider while on order.

In case of completed orders, the API will return an empty response.

Note that this API is rate limited to once per 30 seconds per order, across all IPs. Usage frequency can be increased for an additional charge.

response.name: Error

item.description: These APIs help wwith more complex and involved workflows. Please discuss your use case with the Pidge team before implementing any of these.

item.name: Sandbox APIs

request.description:
You can use this API on **staging only** to simulate GET API hits and order statuses.

Pass the Pidge "id" as a path parameter and the desired status as a query parameter to receive order information with simulated fulfillment information.

Statuses/allowed values for the parameter are listed in order below - these are the same as the [examples of the GET API.]()

"pending"

"processing"

"fulfilled|registered"

"fulfilled|out for pickup"

"fulfilled|reached pickup"

"fulfilled|picked up"

"fulfilled|ofd"

"fulfilled|reached delivery"

"fulfilled|undelivered"

"fulfilled|delivered"

"fulfilled|rto out for delivery"

"fulfilled|rto delivered"

Once you have set up a webhook on the dashboard, you can use this API on **staging only** to simulate webhook hits and order statuses.

Pass the Pidge "id" as a path parameter and the desired status in the body to receive order information with simulated fulfillment information.

Statuses/allowed values for payload are listed in order below - these are the same as the [examples of the GET API.]()

"pending"

"processing"

"fulfilled|registered"

"fulfilled|out for pickup"

"fulfilled|reached pickup"

"fulfilled|picked up"

"fulfilled|ofd"

"fulfilled|reached delivery"

"fulfilled|undelivered"

"fulfilled|delivered"

"fulfilled|rto out for delivery"

"fulfilled|rto delivered"

item.description: These are designed to make testing easy and seamless.
